title: How Long Does a Boudoir Photoshoot Take? A Full Session Day, Hour by Hour
h1: How Long Does a Boudoir Photoshoot Take?
subtitle: An hour-by-hour walk-through of session day at the Mark Andrew studio — from 9 a.m. hair and makeup to a same-day photo reveal by mid-afternoon.
metaDescription: A full session day at the Mark Andrew boudoir studio runs from 9 a.m. to about 3 p.m. — hair, makeup, three outfit changes, a lunch break, and a same-day photo reveal. Here's the hour-by-hour breakdown.
cardTitle: How Long Does a Boudoir Photoshoot Take?
cardExcerpt: A full session day at my studio runs 9 a.m. to about 3 p.m. — hair and makeup, three outfits, a lunch break, and the same-day photo reveal. The hour-by-hour breakdown.
heroAlt: Studio luxury boudoir session — champagne tulle robe, red lace, hours-long shoot day
heroCaption: A full session day, top to bottom — about six hours of transformation
directAnswer: A full boudoir session day at my studio is about six hours, end to end — 9 a.m. arrival, hair and makeup until 10:30, shooting until noon, a one-hour lunch break, then a same-day photo reveal from 1 to 3 p.m. The actual shooting time is roughly 90 minutes; the rest is everything that makes the experience worth coming back for.
closerBody: Don't try to fit this between meetings. Book a full day for yourself. Most clients leave the studio glowing and go out to dinner that night still dressed up. That's by design.

sections.h2: What Does a Full Boudoir Session Day Look Like, Hour by Hour?
sections.answer: 9 a.m. arrival and hair and makeup. 10:30 a.m. wardrobe walk-through and start shooting. 10:30 to noon, three outfit changes and three different sets. Noon to 1 p.m., lunch break. 1 to 3 p.m., photo reveal and ordering appointment. By 3 p.m. you're walking out of the studio with your gallery picked, your album designed, and the work done.
prose: Hair and makeup takes about an hour to ninety minutes. We work with Aletheia Jean, whose work is built specifically for studio lighting — soft glam, bold glam, or maximalist depending on the look you're going for.

Shooting is ninety minutes for three outfits and three sets. We don't rush. You get warmed up in the first set, find your stride by the second, and by the third most clients are giving direction back to me.
The lunch break exists for a reason. You'll need a real moment to step out of the studio energy, eat, drink water, and reset before the reveal. I usually send clients to a spot nearby.

The reveal is the part nobody talks about and the part that changes everything. You'll see 130 to 160 fully edited images projected on a 65-inch TV in 4K. Most clients don't believe what they're seeing the first time.

sections.h2: What's the Longest Part of a Boudoir Session Day?
sections.answer: The longest parts are usually the shoot itself and the reveal — both depend on the client. Some clients warm up to the camera in five minutes; others need fifteen. Some clients pick their gallery and design their album in under an hour at the reveal; others take a full two hours because they love so many images. Both are normal.
prose: I've had clients pick their gallery and finalize an order in under an hour at the reveal. I've had others take the full two hours, debating between two near-identical images. Both are okay. The day is yours.

If you're somebody who needs extra time to feel comfortable in front of the camera, plan on the shoot running closer to two hours than ninety minutes. That's still fully inside the day's timeline — there's room for it.

sections.h2: Do You Offer a Shorter or Express Boudoir Session?
sections.answer: Not for first-time clients. I've tested 90-minute mini-sessions over the last decade, and they almost never produce my best work — most clients are just starting to warm up by the time a mini would be over. I do offer shorter sessions to returning clients who already know my system and don't need the long ramp-up.
prose: The full-day format is a deliberate choice. A boudoir session is a transformation, not a photo shoot. That transformation doesn't happen in ninety minutes — it happens across hours of hair and makeup, conversation, music, multiple sets, and the slow process of getting comfortable in front of the camera.

Past clients are different. They know what to expect, they know my direction style, and they walk in already comfortable. For repeat sessions I can run a tighter timeline and still get the same results.

sections.h2: What Part of the Timing Surprises First-Time Clients the Most?
sections.answer: The most common surprise is how fast the editing is. Most photographers take two to four weeks to deliver a final gallery. At my studio, by the time you come back from lunch at 1 p.m., 130 to 160 of your images are already fully edited — color graded, skin retouched, ready to print. Clients are stunned that what they assumed would take weeks took ninety minutes.
prose: There's a method to the speed: I shoot everything as close to print-ready as possible inside the camera. Color toning, lighting, backdrop wrinkles, light stands out of frame — all of it gets handled while we're shooting, not after. Color grading and skin retouching happen in the hour over lunch. By the time you sit down for the reveal, it's done.

I do this because the high you're on after the shoot is at its peak in the first hour. If you come back two weeks later, the high has faded and the photos hit differently. Same-day reveals capture you while the energy is still in your body.

faqs.q: How long does a boudoir photoshoot take in total?
faqs.a: About six hours, from 9 a.m. arrival to 3 p.m. departure. That includes hair and makeup, three outfit changes, a 90-minute shoot, a lunch break, and a same-day photo reveal.

faqs.q: How long is the actual boudoir photoshoot?
faqs.a: The shooting portion is about 90 minutes, covering three outfit changes and three sets. Total studio time including hair, makeup, and the photo reveal is about six hours.

faqs.q: Can I do a boudoir session in less than a full day?
faqs.a: Not for first-time clients. The full-day format is designed around the transformation a first-timer needs to go through to look and feel their best. Past clients who already know my system can book shorter sessions.

faqs.q: When do I see my boudoir photos after the session?
faqs.a: Same day. After a lunch break, you return to the studio for a photo reveal where 130 to 160 fully edited images are shown on a 65-inch TV. You pick favorites and design your order right there.

social.youtubeDescription:
A full boudoir session day at the Mark Andrew studio is six hours, top to bottom. Most clients are surprised by both how much time it takes and how fast the editing is — they see 130-160 fully edited images at the same-day reveal.

In this guide: the hour-by-hour walk-through of a session day, what takes the longest, why I don't offer mini-sessions for first-timers, and the part that surprises first-time clients most.
